name: wiki-qa
description: Answers questions about a code repository using source file analysis. Use when the user asks a question about how something works, wants to understand a component, or needs help navigating the codebase.
capabilities: Search codebase files to answer questions about implementation details
Provide inline citations linking answers to specific source files and line numbers
Detect question language and respond in the same language
Create Key Files tables mapping files to their roles in the codebase
Synthesize answers from multiple source files for comprehensive understanding
Identify when information is insufficient and suggest files to examine
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 4 potential issues that are all false positives upon manual review. The skill is a legitimate documentation/Q&A tool that reads source code files and answers questions based on file content. No actual code execution, external commands, or cryptographic operations are present.
valueStatement: Stop hunting through files to understand how your code works. This skill reads your source code and provides evidence-based answers with inline citations to specific files and line numbers.
userTitle: Get instant answers about your codebase
limitations: Only answers questions based on existing source files, cannot access external documentation
Does not execute code or test functionality, only analyzes static text
Cannot answer questions about runtime behavior or system dependencies
Limited to files present in the repository at analysis time

useCases.targetUser: Software engineers joining a new project
useCases.title: New developer onboarding
useCases.description: Quickly understand how specific components work by asking natural language questions about the codebase structure and implementation.

useCases.title: Legacy code investigation
useCases.description: Navigate unfamiliar codebases efficiently by asking where features are defined and how different modules interact.

useCases.title: Architecture documentation
useCases.description: Generate explanations of system architecture and component relationships based on actual source code evidence.

promptTemplates.title: Basic code question
promptTemplates.prompt: How does authentication work in this project?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a high-level explanation of a specific feature or component

promptTemplates.title: Find implementation location
promptTemplates.prompt: Where is the user validation logic defined?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to locate specific functions or classes in the codebase

promptTemplates.title: Understand component relationships
promptTemplates.prompt: How do the frontend and backend communicate in this application?
promptTemplates.scenario: When exploring interactions between different parts of the system

promptTemplates.title: Debugging context
promptTemplates.prompt: What could cause the payment processing to fail based on the error handling code?
promptTemplates.scenario: When investigating issues and need to understand error flows and edge cases

bestPractices: Ask specific questions about particular components, functions, or features for the most detailed responses
Review the Key Files table to understand which files are relevant to your question
Follow the inline citations to dive deeper into specific implementation details
Use follow-up questions to explore related components or understand how different parts connect
antiPatterns: Asking questions about topics unrelated to the codebase (this skill only analyzes repository files)
Expecting runtime behavior analysis without looking at test files or execution traces
Assuming answers include information from external documentation or APIs
Requesting code execution or testing functionality (this is read-only analysis)

faq.question: What types of questions can this skill answer?
faq.answer: This skill answers questions about implementation details, file locations, component relationships, and how specific features work based on the source code in your repository.

faq.question: Does this skill execute my code or run tests?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and analyzes source files. It does not execute code, run tests, or access runtime behavior.

faq.question: How accurate are the answers?
faq.answer: Answers are grounded entirely in source code evidence with inline citations. The skill will tell you when information is insufficient rather than guessing.

faq.question: Can this skill understand code in any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill analyzes code as text and can work with any programming language present in your repository.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot find the answer?
faq.answer: The skill will explicitly state when information is insufficient and suggest specific files to examine for more details.

faq.question: Does this skill access external documentation or APIs?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes files within your repository. It does not access external documentation, APIs, or online resources.
